team_context.cap_situation: tight — Colts were over the cap pre-free agency, used restructures (Raimann, Moore) and the Pittman trade to create space. Big money committed to Jones ($88M/2yr), Pierce ($116M/4yr), Gardner, Buckner, Nelson, Raimann, Ward, Bynum. Limited draft capital (no 1st-round picks in 2026 or 2027 due to Gardner trade).
team_context.trade_posture: All-in competing posture after trading two 1sts for Sauce Gardner mid-2025. Team went 8-2 before injuries collapsed season to 8-9. Core is locked in, looking to add depth pieces. Would move declining veterans or redundant pieces for picks but not tearing anything down.

players.name: Daniel Jones
players.performance_evidence: Career renaissance in 2025: 3,101 yards, 19 TD, 8 INT, 68% comp, 100.2 passer rating (9th in NFL), 8.1 Y/A in 13 games. PFF overall grade 71.7. Led Colts to 8-2 start before Achilles tear in Week 14. First in EPA per play. However, torn Achilles in December 2025 adds major uncertainty for 2026 return.

trade_value.reasoning: Just signed a 2-year, $88M deal (largest 2-year deal in NFL history). Strong 2025 production but recovering from Achilles tear. Massive cap hit and dead cap make a trade virtually impossible. Value is theoretical — no one is trading for this contract right now.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed a massive extension and the Colts built their entire 2025-2026 roster around him. Traded two 1sts for Gardner partly because they believed in Jones long-term. Zero chance of a trade.
players.injury_notes: Torn Achilles (December 2025) — underwent surgery, targeting Week 1 2026 return. Also played through broken fibula late in 2025. Prior torn ACL in 2023 with Giants. Significant injury history is a real concern.

players.name: Anthony Richardson Sr.
players.performance_evidence: Lost starting job to Jones in 2025 camp. Played only 2 games in 2025 (9 passing yards total). Career: 17 games, 2,400 passing yards, 47.7% comp in 2024, benched midseason. Fractured orbital bone in Week 6 warmups ended his 2025. 5th-year option will almost certainly be declined.

trade_value.reasoning: Former #4 overall pick with elite physical tools (4.43 40-yard dash, 6'4" 244 lbs) but has been a massive disappointment. Only 17 career games, terrible accuracy, multiple injuries. Still only 24 with rare athleticism, which gives him a lottery-ticket floor of a mid-to-late round pick. A QB-needy team might take a shot.

players.availability_reasoning: Colts have granted Richardson permission to seek a trade. With Jones signed long-term, Richardson has no path to starting in Indy. Team actively looking to move him for whatever they can get.
players.injury_notes: Fractured orbital bone (October 2025), shoulder surgery (2023), shoulder aggravation (2025 OTAs), concussion history. Durability is a major red flag — has missed significant time every season.
players.character_notes: Questions about competitive mentality after voluntarily removing himself from a game in 2024. Requested trade via mutual agreement in February 2026.
players.trade_buzz: NFL Network reported Colts and Richardson mutually agreed to seek a trade in February 2026. Vikings reportedly have interest. Multiple teams likely intrigued by physical tools at a low cost.

players.name: Jonathan Taylor
players.performance_evidence: Led NFL in rushing attempts (323), rushing TDs (18), and total TDs (20) in 2025. 1,585 rushing yards (3rd in NFL), 4.9 YPC, 46 receptions for 378 yards. Named to 3rd Pro Bowl. Had five games with 3+ TDs. Coach Steichen called him 'the best back in the league right now.' Former 2021 rushing champion (1,811 yards).

trade_value.reasoning: Elite RB production but entering final year of deal at age 27 with no guaranteed money remaining. Position devaluation limits trade return. Worth a late 2nd/early 3rd to a contender, but RB market suppresses value. Non-guaranteed salary makes him easy to move on from.

players.availability_reasoning: Cornerstone of offense and Steichen's scheme, but entering final year with $15.5M cap hit and zero guarantees. Extension talks expected but could become available if sides can't agree on new deal terms. Colts may value him but RB is a replaceable position.
players.injury_notes: Missed 2022 with ankle injury, holdout/PUP in 2023. Has been remarkably durable since — played 17 games in each of last two seasons. Efficiency dipped late in 2025 (sub-4.0 YPC in 5 of last 6 games), which could signal wear.
players.trade_buzz: Spotrac projects Taylor toward a $20M/year extension. No active trade buzz, but contract year dynamics always create uncertainty.

players.name: Tyler Goodson
players.performance_evidence: Depth/change-of-pace back behind Taylor. Limited offensive role, primarily a special teams contributor. Restricted free agent re-signed for 2026.

trade_value.reasoning: Depth RB with minimal trade value. Change-of-pace back on a minimum-level deal — essentially no trade market.

players.availability_reasoning: Replaceable depth piece on a cheap deal. Would be included in a trade as salary filler if needed.

players.name: Alec Pierce
players.performance_evidence: Led NFL in yards per catch for 2nd consecutive year (21.3 in 2025, 22.3 in 2024). First 1,000-yard season in 2025 (1,003 yards, 6 TD on just 47 catches). Highest-paid free agent WR in NFL history at $29M/year. Elite deep threat but limited volume — career-high is 47 catches. Over 1/3 of catches went for 20+ yards.

trade_value.reasoning: Young (25) WR on a brand-new 4-year deal with 4 years of control. But the contract is massive ($116M, $84M guaranteed) relative to production (47 catches). The guaranteed money makes him essentially untradeable in the near term — any acquiring team absorbs a huge commitment for a player who's never had more than 47 catches.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed a 4-year, $116M deal days ago. $60M guaranteed at signing, $84M total guaranteed. The Colts traded Pittman specifically to keep Pierce. He's the WR1 for the foreseeable future. Dead cap makes any trade impossible.

players.name: Josh Downs
players.performance_evidence: 58 catches for 566 yards and 4 TDs in 2025, down from 72/803/5 in 2024. PFF grade of 77.1 (25th among 81 WRs). Reliable slot receiver but production dipped in 2025 with Jones's injury and team's late-season collapse. Career: 198 receptions, 2,140 yards in 3 seasons. Size limitations (5'9", 171 lbs) cap ceiling.

trade_value.reasoning: Solid slot receiver on final year of cheap rookie deal. Entering contract year which could increase or decrease value. Size limitations and lack of elite production make him a 4th-round value at best.

players.availability_reasoning: Entering final year of rookie deal. With Pittman traded and Pierce locked in as WR1, Downs becomes important to the offense but could be moved if the Colts draft a WR. Fair value gets it done.
players.injury_notes: Missed time with hip/knee issues and a concussion in 2025. Ankle injury in 2024 preseason.

players.name: Ashton Dulin
players.performance_evidence: Primarily a special teams ace and depth WR. Jones connected with him on a 48-yard play in 2025. Limited offensive role but has shown big-play ability when targeted. Re-signed as a veteran presence.

trade_value.reasoning: Special teams contributor and WR4/5 on most rosters. Minimal trade value as a depth piece.

players.availability_reasoning: Depth receiver on a cheap deal. Would move for any reasonable offer, but unlikely to attract interest.

players.name: Tyler Warren
players.performance_evidence: Franchise-record 817 receiving yards by a rookie TE. 76 receptions, 4 receiving TDs, 1 rushing TD in 17 games. Started all 17 games, played 6th-most snaps among all NFL TEs. Named Pro Bowl replacement for Brock Bowers. Led all rookie TEs in receptions and yards. Elite versatility as receiver and blocker. GM Ballard said 'He's going to get better, and that's the exciting thing.'

trade_value.reasoning: 23-year-old Pro Bowl-caliber TE on a rookie deal with 4 years of control including 5th-year option. Top-15 pick production immediately. Young, cheap, elite trajectory. Worth a top-10 pick equivalent — teams would give up a 1st+ for this kind of asset.

players.availability_reasoning: Cornerstone of the offense on a rookie deal. 23 years old, franchise-record rookie season, beloved in the locker room. Ballard and Steichen have publicly praised his ceiling. No conceivable scenario where Colts trade him.
players.injury_notes: Iron man as a rookie — played all 17 games. Only missed time briefly with an illness in Week 13.

players.name: Will Mallory
players.performance_evidence: Depth TE behind Warren and behind Mo Alie-Cox in snap counts. Limited offensive production. Blocking-focused role.

trade_value.reasoning: Depth TE on final year of rookie deal. Essentially no trade value.

players.availability_reasoning: Depth piece that could be moved without any impact to the roster.

players.name: Bernhard Raimann
players.performance_evidence: PFF pass-blocking grade of 82.0 (14th among tackles) and run-blocking grade of 80.7 (10th) in 2024. One of just 5 OTs to average 80+ in both pass and run blocking over past 2 seasons per PFF. Signed a 4-year, $100M extension in July 2025 ($60M guaranteed). Just restructured to create $8.25M cap space. Top-5 paid LT in the NFL.

trade_value.reasoning: 28-year-old above-average LT on a long-term deal with 4 years remaining. Premium position value. But massive dead cap ($31M) after restructure makes trade virtually impossible in 2026.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed a $100M extension and restructured his deal to help the team's cap. $31M dead cap if traded makes it financially impossible. Franchise LT for the foreseeable future.
players.injury_notes: Missed 1 game with concussion and 2 with knee injury in 2024. Generally durable.

players.name: Quenton Nelson
players.performance_evidence: PFF's #4 rated guard entering 2026. Named to 2026 Pro Bowl (career total of 7 Pro Bowls). 4x First-Team All-Pro. Widely considered the best guard of his generation. Still elite despite turning 30. Spotrac projects 4yr/$92M extension ($23M/year).

trade_value.reasoning: Still elite at 30, but entering contract year with a $24.2M cap hit and only $6.2M dead cap. IOL position limits the premium. Worth a late 1st/early 2nd in a vacuum, but age and expiring deal reduce it. Extension candidate — Colts likely to extend rather than trade.

players.availability_reasoning: Franchise icon and locker room leader, but entering contract year at age 30 with a massive cap hit. Only $6.2M dead cap means the Colts could save ~$18M by trading him. Extension expected, but if talks stall, his trade value is significant given his elite play.
players.injury_notes: Elbow injury caused him to miss 1 game in 2025. Has dealt with various injuries in recent years but has been largely available.

players.name: Tanor Bortolini
players.performance_evidence: Took over as starting center following Ryan Kelly's retirement (after 2024 in Minnesota). Started most games in 2025. Adequate starter but not a standout. Missed 1 game with concussion.

trade_value.reasoning: Young starting center on a cheap final-year rookie deal. Below-average starter but cheap and developing. Worth a late 5th at best.

players.availability_reasoning: If Colts draft a center, Bortolini becomes expendable. Entering final year of rookie deal with modest production.
players.injury_notes: Concussion caused him to miss 1 game in 2025.

players.name: Matt Goncalves
players.performance_evidence: Second-year guard who stepped into starting role. Adequate starter developing alongside Nelson. Cheap rookie deal with 3 years of control.

trade_value.reasoning: Young starting guard on a cheap rookie deal. Not a standout but has years of control. Worth a late 5th/6th due to contract value.

players.availability_reasoning: Young, cheap starter with years of control. Colts need OL depth and wouldn't move him cheaply, but a mid-round pick could get it done.

players.name: Jalen Travis
players.performance_evidence: Rookie 4th-round pick from Iowa State who played well enough in Braden Smith's absence (4 games missed) that the Colts let Smith walk in free agency. Became the starting RT. Has 4 years of cheap control.

trade_value.reasoning: Young starting RT on a rookie deal with 4 years of control. Stepped into starting role as a rookie. Premium position on a cheap deal. Worth a mid-to-late 4th.

players.availability_reasoning: Young, cheap starting RT the Colts invested in by letting Braden Smith leave. They view Travis as the future at RT. Would need an overpay to move him.

players.name: JT Tuimoloau
players.performance_evidence: Underwhelming rookie season: 16 tackles across 12 appearances (215 defensive snaps). Zero sacks despite 12.5 sacks in final college season at Ohio State. Was a healthy scratch Week 1. Had an oblique injury in Week 16. Limited snaps behind Latu, Paye, and Ebukam. Expected to take on larger role in 2026 with Paye and Ebukam departing.

trade_value.reasoning: Former 5-star recruit and 2nd-round pick with elite college production but quiet NFL debut. 3 years of cheap control at a premium position. Upside play — worth a late 3rd/early 4th based on pedigree, age, and contract. Role should expand significantly in 2026.

players.availability_reasoning: Colts are thin at EDGE after losing Paye and Ebukam in free agency. They need Tuimoloau to step up. Unlikely to move him unless they get significant value, but his underwhelming rookie year means the premium isn't as high as his draft status suggests.
players.injury_notes: Oblique injury in Week 16 vs 49ers. Otherwise healthy.

players.name: Grover Stewart
players.performance_evidence: Veteran run-stuffing NT. Had 49 tackles, 0.5 sack, 1 INT through 15 games in 2025. PFF noted him as a 'glue guy' for the defense. Spotrac noted the Colts could free up $12.25M by moving on from Stewart, who is non-guaranteed in 2026. Had a down year statistically.

trade_value.reasoning: 30-year-old run-stuffing DT on an expiring deal with a $14.25M cap hit. Zero dead cap essentially. A contender needing run defense might offer a late-round pick, but the cap hit limits the market.

players.availability_reasoning: Non-guaranteed $14.25M cap hit in a contract year at age 30. Spotrac specifically identified Stewart as a cut/trade candidate to save $12.25M. Colts signed multiple DT reinforcements (Clemons, Nnadi, traded for Wooden). Writing is on the wall.
players.trade_buzz: Spotrac identified him as a release candidate. Beat reporters have noted his production declined and his contract is not justified by 2025 output.

players.name: DeForest Buckner
players.performance_evidence: 3x Pro Bowl, 1x First-Team All-Pro (2020). PFF grade of 74 in 2025 (14th among DL). 4 sacks, 36 pressures, 11 QB hits in 10 starts before neck injury (herniated disc). Missed 7 games total in 2025 — placed on IR twice (neck). Had surgery. Only 31 at time but significant neck concerns. GM Ballard expects return for 2026.

trade_value.reasoning: Former elite interior pass rusher but turning 32 with a $26.6M cap hit, $20.8M dead cap, and serious neck injury concerns. Massive dead cap makes a trade extremely costly for the Colts. When healthy still very productive, but the injury risk is substantial.

players.availability_reasoning: Enormous cap hit ($26.6M) in what could be his final season. $20.8M dead cap discourages a trade but restructure is more likely. GM Ballard publicly committed to Buckner returning but the neck injury (herniated disc, surgery) is a wildcard. If healthy, he's a core piece. If not, they may need to absorb the dead cap.
players.injury_notes: Herniated disc in neck — placed on IR twice in 2025, underwent neck surgery. Considered retirement briefly. Also missed 6 games with ankle injury in 2024. Serious health concerns for a 32-year-old.
players.character_notes: Team leader and defensive captain. Extremely respected in the locker room.
players.trade_buzz: Beat reporters have discussed restructuring his deal rather than trading or cutting. Ballard expects him at training camp.

players.name: Laiatu Latu
players.performance_evidence: Major second-year breakout: 8.5 sacks (CBS), 11 sacks (PFF), 45 tackles, 3 INTs, 5 pass breakups, 1 forced fumble in 16 games. PFF overall grade of 84.1 (10th among edge defenders). 61 pressures (15th among EDGE per PFF). Started all 16 games he played. Huge leap from 4 sacks as a rookie. Undisputed top pass rusher for the Colts.

trade_value.reasoning: 25-year-old ascending EDGE rusher on a rookie deal with 3 years of control (plus 5th-year option). Premium position, Pro Bowl-caliber production in year 2, elite PFF grade. Young EDGE rushers on cheap deals are the most valuable trade assets in football. Worth multiple 1st-round picks in practice.

players.availability_reasoning: Ascending 25-year-old EDGE on a rookie deal. The Colts' pass rush is built around him, especially with Paye and Ebukam gone. He's the defensive cornerstone for the future alongside Sauce Gardner. There is no price the Colts would accept.
players.injury_notes: Neck injury history from college (Washington, required surgery in 2021, sat out entire season before transferring to UCLA). Has been healthy in the NFL so far.

players.name: Segun Olubi
players.performance_evidence: Restricted free agent re-tendered for 2026. Stepped into a starting role after Zaire Franklin's departure. Special teams contributor who earned defensive snaps. Developing player at a position of need for the Colts.

trade_value.reasoning: Undrafted LB who earned a starting role but limited proven production. On a cheap deal. Worth a late-round pick at most.

players.availability_reasoning: If Colts draft a LB (a major need after Franklin's trade), Olubi could become expendable. Cheap enough that he's not blocking anything.

players.name: Austin Ajiake
players.performance_evidence: 32 tackles and 2 QB hits through 16 games in 2025. Primarily depth/special teams role. Not expected to see significant starting time.

trade_value.reasoning: Depth LB with minimal trade value. Special teams contributor on a minimum deal.

players.availability_reasoning: Depth piece that the Colts would move without hesitation for any draft pick.

players.name: Sauce Gardner
players.performance_evidence: 2x First-Team All-Pro (2022, 2023), NFL Defensive Rookie of the Year (2022). Only CB since 1970 to be named first-team All-Pro in each of first 2 seasons. In press-man coverage over his career: 52 targets in 382 snaps, 56.1 passer rating against. However, passer rating against has risen for 3 consecutive seasons (62.7 in 2022 to 98.1 in 2025). Played only 7 games with Jets and a handful with Colts in 2025 due to trade timing and calf injury.

trade_value.reasoning: 25-year-old 2x All-Pro CB on a long-term deal through 2030 with a very team-friendly $9.5M cap hit in 2026. The Colts just traded TWO 1st-round picks for him. Elite pedigree, prime age, 5 years of control. Worth more than any single draft pick.

players.availability_reasoning: The Colts traded two 1st-round picks (2026, 2027) and WR Adonai Mitchell for Gardner in November 2025. He is THE reason they have no 1st-round picks. Trading him would be organizational malpractice. He's 25 with 5 years of control on a deal with a low 2026 cap hit. Absolutely untouchable.
players.injury_notes: Calf injury limited him to only a handful of games with the Colts after the trade. Also missed time with hamstring in 2024 with Jets. Has not played a full 17-game season since 2023. Full secondary rarely played together in 2025.

players.name: Charvarius Ward
players.performance_evidence: Former All-Pro and Super Bowl champion (KC). PFF grade of 77.1 in 2025 (9th among CBs) despite playing only 7 games. 25 tackles, 7 passes defensed, fumble recovery. When healthy, shadowed opposing WR1s effectively. 2023 All-Pro with 49ers (23 PDs, 5 INTs). But only played 7 games in 2025 due to 3 concussions.

trade_value.reasoning: When healthy, a true CB1 who is elite in man coverage. But 3 concussions in 2025 with retirement consideration, $19.9M cap hit, and $18.3M dead cap make him virtually untradeable. The dead cap exceeds the cap hit. Any trade would COST the Colts money.

players.availability_reasoning: Ward himself said he was 'strongly considering retirement' after 3 concussions in 2025. Recent reports suggest he's 'reconsidering that stance' and may play. But if he decides to retire, the Colts get cap relief. The $18.3M dead cap makes a trade nonsensical — you'd eat almost as much dead cap as keeping him. The realistic outcome is he either plays or retires.
players.injury_notes: 3 documented concussions in 2025 — the most significant factor in his valuation. Placed on IR twice. Described lingering symptoms as 'traumatic.' Was contemplating retirement. Also dealt with personal tragedy (1-year-old daughter died in October 2024).
players.character_notes: Deeply respected veteran. Super Bowl champion with Chiefs. Personal tragedy has understandably affected him. Team and coaches publicly support whatever decision he makes.
players.trade_buzz: No trade buzz. The story is entirely about whether he plays or retires. GM Ballard said at the Combine that Ward's 'frame of mind is really good' and 'all indications is he's wanting to move forward and play.'

players.name: Kenny Moore II
players.performance_evidence: 2021 Pro Bowl selection. One of the NFL's best slot corners for years. Had 44 tackles, 1 INT returned for TD, 6 PDs through 15 games in 2025. Restructured his contract midseason to help facilitate the Gardner trade. 9th-year veteran. Walter Payton Man of the Year nominee. Still productive but entering his 30s.

trade_value.reasoning: 30-year-old slot CB in a contract year with a $13.1M cap hit and only $6M dead cap. Good player but positional value of nickel CB limits trade return. A contender might offer a 4th/5th for a proven slot corner. The Colts could save ~$7M by trading him.

players.availability_reasoning: Entering final year of deal at age 30 with $13.1M cap hit. Only $6M dead cap means Colts save ~$7M by moving him. With Gardner and Ward (if healthy) locked in as outside CBs, Moore is valuable but not irreplaceable. Beloved leader though, so they'd want fair value.
players.injury_notes: Calf injury caused him to miss time early in 2025 season.
players.character_notes: Walter Payton Man of the Year nominee. Locker room leader and team captain. Hugely respected.

players.name: Daniel Scott
players.performance_evidence: Stepped into a starting safety role alongside Bynum. Depth/rotational player who earned more playing time in 2025. On final year of rookie deal.

trade_value.reasoning: Young starting safety on a cheap deal but limited proven production. Worth a late-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Entering final year of rookie deal. Colts may address safety in the draft. Expendable if a better option emerges.

players.name: Cam Bynum
players.performance_evidence: Led Colts with 4 INTs and 8 passes defended through 15 games in 2025, plus 72 tackles. Signed a 4-year, $60M deal in March 2025 after starring in Minnesota. Immediately became a defensive leader. Played alongside Ward and Moore in an elite secondary when healthy.

trade_value.reasoning: 27-year-old starting safety on a 3-year deal with strong production. Ball-hawking FS who led the Colts in INTs. But $18M dead cap exceeds the $15M cap hit, making a trade financially irrational for the Colts. Essentially locked in.

players.availability_reasoning: Signed a $60M deal just one year ago. Dead cap ($18M) exceeds cap hit ($15M). Colts invested heavily in the secondary and Bynum is a centerpiece of it. Zero chance of a trade.

players.name: DJ Giddens
players.performance_evidence: Kansas State product drafted in the 4th round of the 2025 NFL Draft. Productive college runner with good size and vision — rushed for over 1,400 yards in his final college season. As a rookie entering 2025, expected to serve as the RB2 behind Jonathan Taylor, providing complementary carries and special teams contributions. Has not yet established an NFL track record but flashed in preseason/limited action.

trade_value.reasoning: Day-3 rookie RB with 4 years of cheap control but unproven at the NFL level. RB is a low-value position for trades. Worth roughly a 6th-round pick based on his draft capital and potential but lack of production data.

players.availability_reasoning: Colts invested a 4th-round pick in him recently and need RB depth behind Jonathan Taylor. They wouldn't move him cheaply, but a surprising offer of a 4th or 5th could get the conversation started given the team's other roster needs.

players.name: Mo Alie-Cox
players.performance_evidence: Long-time Colts tight end, former VCU basketball player who converted to football. Primarily an inline blocker and red-zone target. Has never exceeded 35 receptions or 400 yards in a season. Caught 10-20 passes in recent seasons as TE2 behind Jelani Woods and others. Valued more for blocking than receiving. At 32, his athletic traits have diminished, and he's a depth/veteran presence piece.

trade_value.reasoning: 32-year-old blocking TE on an expiring deal with minimal receiving production. Essentially no trade value — a team needing a blocking TE could sign a similar player off the street. Worth a conditional 7th at best.

players.availability_reasoning: Aging veteran on an expiring deal with no dead cap. Colts have younger TEs they're developing. If any team called with a late-round pick, Indianapolis would accommodate the move. More likely a camp/in-season cut candidate than a trade piece.

players.name: Jaylon Carlies
players.performance_evidence: 2nd-round pick out of Missouri in 2024. Played as a rookie in 2024 and earned a starting role by the end of the season, showing versatility as a modern linebacker who can cover and play near the line of scrimmage. Former safety/linebacker tweener in college which gives him plus coverage ability for the position. Still developing as a run defender and in play recognition. Showed enough to hold LB1 duties entering year two but remains a work in progress — likely in the 50th-60th percentile range among starting LBs league-wide.

trade_value.reasoning: Young 2nd-round LB on a cheap rookie deal with 3 years of control. Still developing but has starting-caliber traits and coveted coverage ability. Worth a mid-3rd to early 4th — age and contract upside prop up the value beyond current production.

players.availability_reasoning: Colts invested a 2nd-round pick in him just one year ago and are counting on him as a building block at LB. They would need a clear overpay (early-to-mid 2nd) to consider moving their young starting linebacker. Not being shopped.

players.name: John Bullock
players.performance_evidence: Undrafted free agent who earned a roster spot with Indianapolis. Limited NFL track record — likely competing for LB2 duties and contributing on special teams. College production (likely from a mid-major or Group of Five school) earned him an UDFA opportunity but he is unproven as a consistent NFL starter. Holding the LB2 slot suggests he impressed in camp/preseason but has not established himself as a reliable every-down starter.

trade_value.reasoning: UDFA linebacker with minimal track record and no draft pedigree. Cheap contract is the main appeal but there's no proven production to generate trade interest. Worth a conditional 7th at best.

players.availability_reasoning: As an UDFA with no dead cap and no organizational investment beyond a minimum deal, the Colts would move him for any reasonable draft compensation. He's replaceable depth/special teams player.

players.name: Juanyeh Thomas
players.performance_evidence: Georgia Tech product who went undrafted in 2022 and bounced around before landing a role with the Colts. Has developed into a capable strong safety — known more for physicality in the box and run support than coverage range. Started games in 2024-2025, performing as a below-average to average starter at the position. Special teams contributor as well. Not a game-changer but functional enough to hold the starting SS job in Indianapolis's defense.

trade_value.reasoning: Below-average starting safety on an expiring minimum-level deal with no dead cap. Safety is already a low-value trade position, and his limited pedigree and one year of control make him worth a 6th-round pick at most.

players.availability_reasoning: Expiring deal, no dead cap, and the Colts could look to upgrade at SS. If a team offered a 6th or 7th round pick, Indianapolis would likely accommodate the trade. Not being actively shopped but there's no reason to block a deal.
